name: ocr-image-to-markdown
description: Use when the source material is a screenshot, scanned page, image-only document, table snapshot, slide image, or other visual content that must be transcribed into Markdown before further processing, especially when no command-based OCR path is available or when a workflow needs a multimodal fallback for image-based source ingestion.

triggers: A screenshot, scan, or image-only document must be turned into Markdown
Command-based OCR is unavailable or unsuitable
The PPT workflow is blocked at source normalization because the source is image-based
do_not_use_when: The source is already normal text, Markdown, HTML, or a standard PDF that a canonical converter can process
The task only needs a high-level visual summary instead of Markdown transcription

capabilities: Transcribe image-based source material into structured Markdown
Preserve headings, lists, tables, and reading order
Act as a multimodal fallback for workflow source ingestion
Prepare project-local Markdown handoff artifacts for downstream workflow stages
constraints: Do not invent text that is not visible in the source image
Do not flatten recoverable structure into plain paragraphs
Mark uncertainty explicitly when parts of the image are unreadable
